story_prompt: create a lecture on javascript function

scenes.text: Introduction to JavaScript Functions: Functions are fundamental building blocks in JavaScript. They are sets of statements that perform tasks or calculate values. In this scene, we'll briefly introduce what a function is and its role in programming.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Introduction]; B --> C[Concept of Functions]; C --> D[End];

scenes.text: Defining a Function: To define a function in JavaScript, you use the 'function' keyword, followed by a name and a code block. This scene covers the syntax and structure of defining functions.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Function Definition]; B --> C[Assign Parameters]; C --> D[End];

scenes.text: Function Invocation: Functions need to be called or invoked to execute the code inside them. We will explore different ways to invoke functions and what happens when they are called.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Invoke Function]; B --> C[Execute Code]; C --> D[End];

scenes.text: Return Values: Functions can return values using the 'return' statement. This scene will detail how return values work and their significance within the function.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Function Execution]; B --> C[Return Value]; C --> D[Use Return Value]; D --> E[End];

scenes.text: Types of Functions: JavaScript supports various types of functions including regular functions, arrow functions, and anonymous functions. In this concluding scene, we'll summarize these types and their use cases.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Types of Functions]; B --> C[Regular]; B --> D[Arrow]; B --> E[Anonymous]; E --> F[End];
